Tool_child_education_LearnText: Education costs rise faster than general inflation. Enter today’s fee estimate, years until needed, education inflation, and expected investment return — get future cost and the SIP via sipRequired.
Tool_child_education_FormulaNote: Education inflation in India is often assumed around 8–12%. Returns are illustrative, not guaranteed.
Tool_child_education_CostHint: Today’s total for the course / degree you have in mind.
Tool_child_education_RateHint: Expected annual return on the education SIP.
Tool_child_education_ScenarioSubtitle: Same future cost, different SIP returns
Tool_child_education_ExampleStep_4: Lower return needs a higher SIP; higher return lowers it.
Tool_child_education_ExampleStep_5: Review cost and inflation every few years as fees change.
Tool_child_education_Step_1: Enter today’s education cost and years left.
Tool_child_education_Info_Purpose: Estimate future education cost and the SIP to fund it.
Tool_child_education_Info_WhyKnow: Waiting until admission year makes fees feel sudden and unaffordable.
Tool_child_education_Info_RealLife: Parents planning for college, IIT/IIM, or study abroad.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When you already know the target corpus.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Goal_When: For any goal amount without inflation on cost.
Tool_child_education_Info_Alt_Edu_When: If you will borrow part of the fee.
Tool_child_education_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra inflates education cost then applies sipRequired in one flow.
Tool_child_education_Info_Motto: Inflate the fee — then fund the SIP.
Tool_80g_donation_Subtitle: உங்கள் ஸ்லாபில் விலக்கு மற்றும் வரி சேமிப்பு
Tool_80g_donation_LearnText: Section 80G (old regime) lets you claim 50% or 100% of eligible donations. Some donations are capped at 10% of adjusted income. Enter amount, eligibility, limit flag, income, and slab to see deduction and approximate tax saving.
Tool_80g_donation_FormulaNote: Illustrative only — check the trust’s 80G category and keep receipts. New regime generally does not allow 80G.
Tool_80g_donation_EligHint: Depends on the approved fund / trust category.
Tool_80g_donation_LimitHint: Some 80G donations have a 10% of AGI qualifying limit.
Tool_80g_donation_ExampleStep_1: 10% of income = 1,20,000; donation 50,000 is within limit.
Tool_80g_donation_ExampleStep_5: If 100% eligible, deduction and saving would double.
Tool_80g_donation_Step_2: If limited, base = min(donation, 10% of income).
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Purpose: Estimate 80G deduction and tax saving on charitable donations.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong category or limit assumptions overstate the tax benefit.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_RealLife: Donations to approved trusts, PM CARES, and notified funds (old regime).
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For full-year tax after all deductions.
Tool_80g_donation_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra models 50%/100% eligibility and the optional 10% income cap with slab saving.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_LearnText: Cost Inflation Index (CII) adjusts the purchase cost of a long-term capital asset for inflation. Indexed cost = original cost × (CII of the year of transfer ÷ CII of the year of acquisition). This reduces taxable long-term capital gains under the older indexation rules.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_FormulaNote: CII values are notified by the Income Tax Department each year (base FY 2001-02 = 100). This tool includes a small recent-year table for illustration — confirm the official notification for your FY.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_YearHint: Pick FYs from the built-in CII table (recent years).
Tool_cost_inflation_index_ExampleStep_4: If sale price is ₹20 lakh, indexed gain ≈ 20L − 12.96L.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_ExampleStep_5: Confirm whether indexation still applies for your asset and FY under current law.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_2: Select buy and sell financial years.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_3: Look up CII for each year from the table.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Step_4: Indexed cost = cost × sell CII ÷ buy CII.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Purpose: Compute inflation-adjusted cost for long-term capital gains.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_WhyKnow: Indexation can materially cut taxable LTCG on property and other assets where it still applies.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_RealLife: Selling a house bought years ago; estimating indexed cost before filing ITR.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_1: Find indexed cost from buy and sell CII
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_2: Compare how year choice changes the factor
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Goal_3: Use a recent CII reference table
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Cg_Wh: When you need tax on the gain after indexed cost.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Tax_W: For overall tax liability including other income.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Tax_E: Salary + capital gains in one return.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Alt_Prope: When buying property and estimating registration cost.
Stamp duty on a new flat.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Different: FinYatra focuses on indexed cost with a built-in recent CII table and year scenarios.
Tool_cost_inflation_index_Info_Motto: Inflation-adjust your cost before you tax the gain.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_LearnText: Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP) is a government savings certificate that doubles your investment over a notified tenure. At about 7.5%, that tenure is typically 115 months (~9 years 7 months). Interest is taxable; there is no 80C benefit on the deposit.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_FormulaNote: Maturity = 2 × principal. At 7.5% the notified doubling period is 115 months; for other rates we approximate tenure from compound doubling time.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_RateHint: Default ~7.5% with 115-month doubling; change rate to see tenure shift.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ScenarioSubtitle: Same principal — tenure changes with rate
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ExampleStep_4: Interest earned = ₹1,00,000 (taxable as per your slab).
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_ExampleStep_5: Compare with PPF/NSC if you need tax-free or 80C benefits.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Step_2: Enter or keep the notified rate (~7.5%).
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Step_3: Tenure is 115 months at 7.5%, else approx from doubling formula.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Purpose: See when your KVP doubles and how much interest you earn.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_WhyKnow: KVP is simple (money doubles) but interest is taxable and tenure is long.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_RealLife: Parking a lump sum in a post-office certificate; comparing with bank FD.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Ppf_When: When you want EEE tax-free compounding and 80C.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Nsc_Cons: Does not literally double in one tenure.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you need flexible tenure or premature withdrawal.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Rate and safety vary by bank.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows doubling maturity and rate-linked tenure with clear taxable-interest note.
Tool_kisan_vikas_patra_Info_Motto: Simple doubling — know the wait and the tax.
Tool_mahila_samman_LearnText: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate (MSSC) is a small-savings scheme for women with an illustrative rate around 7.5% and a 2-year tenure. The maximum deposit is typically ₹2 lakh. This tool estimates interest payout and maturity on your deposit (capped at the limit).
Tool_mahila_samman_FormulaNote: Illustrative: total interest ≈ deposit × rate × 2 years; deposit capped at ₹2 lakh. Actual compounding/payout rules follow the scheme notification.
Tool_mahila_samman_CappedNote: Amount above ₹2 lakh is ignored for this estimate.
Tool_mahila_samman_ExampleStep_5: Compare with a 2-year bank FD at similar rates.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Purpose: Estimate interest and maturity on a women’s small-savings deposit.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_WhyKnow: Short 2-year tenure and ₹2L cap make it useful for parking surplus safely.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_RealLife: Woman investor parking up to ₹2 lakh for 2 years at a sovereign rate.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you need more than ₹2 lakh or flexible tenure.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Rate may be lower than MSSC.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Alt_Scss_When: When you are a senior and want quarterly income.
Tool_mahila_samman_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra models the ₹2L cap, 2-year interest payout, and deposit scenarios for MSSC.
Tool_annuity_LearnText: An annuity turns a retirement corpus into a regular pension. For a forever (perpetuity) payout, monthly pension ≈ corpus × annual rate ÷ 12. For a finite tenure, we use the standard annuity payment formula so the corpus is exhausted over the chosen years.
Tool_annuity_FormulaNote: Illustrative rates only — real annuity quotes depend on age, insurer, and product (fixed vs increasing). NPS often requires a portion of corpus to buy an annuity.
Tool_annuity_YearsHint: Finite annuity depletes the corpus over this period.
Tool_annuity_Result_PerpetuityFootnote: Corpus preserved in theory at this rate
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_3: Finite 20 years: use annuity PMT on monthly rate.
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_4: Finite monthly is higher than perpetuity because corpus is drawn down.
Tool_annuity_ExampleStep_5: Compare insurer quotes before buying an annuity.
Tool_annuity_Step_1: Enter corpus and assumed annuity rate.
Tool_annuity_Step_4: Finite: monthly from ordinary annuity formula.
Tool_annuity_Info_Purpose: Estimate monthly pension from a retirement corpus.
Tool_annuity_Info_WhyKnow: Helps size how much corpus you need for a target pension.
Tool_annuity_Info_RealLife: NPS exit annuity; buying a life annuity with retirement savings.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Nps_When: When building corpus with mandatory annuity at exit.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Retirement_When: When you need corpus target and SIP to get there.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Swp_When: When withdrawing from mutual funds instead of buying an annuity.
Tool_annuity_Info_Alt_Swp_Example: Monthly SWP from a debt/hybrid fund.
Tool_annuity_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows both perpetuity and finite annuity PMT from the same corpus and rate.
Tool_annuity_Info_Motto: Turn corpus into a clear monthly pension estimate.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_LearnText: Loan and deposit quotes mix nominal, effective, monthly, and annual rates. Effective annual rate (EAR) = (1 + r/n)^n − 1. Monthly rate is often annual ÷ 12. Converting puts offers on the same footing before you compare EMIs.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_FormulaNote: Nominal → EAR uses compounding frequency n. Monthly ↔ annual here is the simple ×12 / ÷12 conversion; EAR from monthly compounds (1+m)^12 − 1.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_ExampleStep: Use EAR when comparing loans or deposits with different compounding.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Step_2: Enter the rate and compounds per year if needed.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Purpos: Convert between nominal, effective, monthly, and annual rates.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_WhyKno: Apples-to-apples rate comparison avoids misleading loan or FD quotes.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_RealLi: Comparing card EMI monthly % with a personal loan APR; FD compounding.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Alt_Em: When you already have an annual rate and need EMI.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Alt_Fl: When a dealer quotes flat rate vs bank reducing rate.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Differ: FinYatra converts nominal↔effective and monthly↔annual in one place with compounding scenarios.
Tool_interest_rate_converter_Info_Motto: Compare rates only after you convert them.
Tool_lta_LearnText: Leave Travel Allowance (LTA) can be partly exempt under Section 10(5) when you travel on leave. Rules are detailed (fare type, family, destination). A common simplification: up to 2 journeys in a 4-year block. Taxable LTA = max(0, claimed − exempt).
Tool_lta_FormulaNote: Enter the exempt amount you expect (based on eligible fare). This tool does not verify fare rules — it estimates taxable excess. Note: typically 2 trips in a 4-year block.
Tool_lta_ExemptHint: Usually limited to eligible travel fare for self/family as per rules.
Tool_lta_TripsHint: Simplified: up to {0} trips in a {1}-year block. Trips outside 1–{0} get ₹0 exempt here.
Tool_lta_ExampleStep_2: One trip used in the 4-year block (within 2-trip limit).
Tool_lta_ExampleStep_5: Keep tickets and employer proofs for the exemption claim.
Tool_lta_Step_3: Note trips in the 4-year block (max 2 simplified).
Tool_lta_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much of your LTA claim may be taxable.
Tool_lta_Info_WhyKnow: Over-claiming beyond eligible fare creates taxable salary income.
Tool_lta_Info_RealLife: Domestic leave travel reimbursement from employer; block-year planning.
Tool_lta_Info_Goal_1: Split claimed LTA into exempt vs taxable
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Hra_When: When estimating house rent allowance exemption.
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Salary_When: When modelling full CTC and take-home.
Tool_lta_Info_Alt_Tax_When: When taxable LTA flows into annual tax.
Tool_lta_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra estimates exempt vs taxable LTA with a clear 2-trips-in-4-years note.
Tool_lta_Info_Motto: Claim LTA — know what stays tax-free.
Tool_rental_income_tax_LearnText: For let-out house property, net annual value is annual rent minus municipal taxes. A flat 30% standard deduction applies on NAV. The result is taxable house-property income — estimated here at your marginal slab.
Tool_rental_income_tax_FormulaNote: Educational estimate. Ignores interest on home loan (Sec 24b), vacancy, and stacking with other income. Confirm with a CA for filing.
Tool_rental_income_tax_VerdictNote: Taxable house-property income is about {0}. Estimated tax at your slab is {1} ({2}).
Tool_rental_income_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: How tax changes with slab or higher rent
Tool_rental_income_tax_ExampleStep_5: At 30% slab, estimated tax ≈ ₹73,080 (before cess/other income).
Tool_rental_income_tax_Step_1: Enter annual rent and municipal taxes paid.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Step_4: Estimate tax at your marginal slab.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate taxable rental income after municipal taxes and the 30% standard deduction.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Landlords often overestimate tax by forgetting the 30% deduction, or underestimate by ignoring municipal taxes.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_RealLife: Let-out flat, second home rented out, comparing net yield after tax.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Alt_Hra_When: When you are a tenant claiming HRA, not a landlord.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To see full ITR with salary and other income.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Alt_Rent_Whe: For generating rent receipts as a tenant.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra shows NAV, 30% deduction, and slab tax in one landlord-focused view.
Tool_rental_income_tax_Info_Motto: Know the taxable rent — not just the rent cheque.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_LearnText: EPF is generally EEE after 5 years of continuous service. Withdrawing earlier can make interest (and sometimes contributions) taxable. This tool uses a simplified interest-portion model for education — not a substitute for Form 31 / EPFO rules.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Simplified: ≥5 years → fully exempt; <5 years → assumed interest % of balance is taxable at your slab. Real rules depend on employment continuity, TDS u/s 192A, and Form 15G/15H.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_InterestHint: Used only when service < 5 years. Default 25% is illustrative.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Exempt: Service ≥ 5 years — withdrawal is generally tax-free (EEE).
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Taxable: Service under 5 years — interest portion treated as taxable (simplified).
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Note_Unemployed: Unemployed with <5 years — still showing taxable interest estimate for planning.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_ScenarioSubtitle: Tax if you wait vs withdraw early
Tool_epf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_5: If you wait until 5 years, the same balance is generally fully exempt.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Step_1: Enter balance and years of continuous service.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much of an EPF withdrawal may be taxable before vs after 5 years.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: Premature EPF withdrawal can create an unexpected tax bill. Knowing the 5-year rule helps timing.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Job change, unemployment claim, retirement planning, Form 31 withdrawal.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Epf_When: To project future EPF balance while contributing.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To see total tax with salary income.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Alt_Tax_Example: Add taxable EPF interest to gross.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra highlights the 5-year rule with a clear taxable vs tax-free split.
Tool_epf_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Wait for five years when you can — EEE is powerful.
Tool_xirr_LearnText: XIRR is the annualised return when you invest on different dates. Mutual fund statements and SIPs with lumpsums use XIRR. Enter investments as negative amounts and the current/final value as positive.
Tool_xirr_FormulaNote: Solved with Newton–Raphson so that the NPV of all cashflows at rate r equals zero. Needs at least one inflow and one outflow.
Tool_xirr_FinalHint: Current portfolio value or redemption proceeds (positive).
Tool_xirr_VerdictNote: XIRR is about {0}. Absolute profit/loss is {1}.
Tool_xirr_VerdictFail: Could not solve XIRR. Use negative investments and a positive final value with valid dates.
Tool_xirr_ScenarioSubtitle: How XIRR moves if the portfolio ends higher or lower
Tool_xirr_ExampleStep_5: The resulting r is your annualised XIRR.
Tool_xirr_Step_2: Enter final date and positive final value.
Tool_xirr_Info_Abbreviation: XIRR — Extended Internal Rate of Return
Tool_xirr_Info_Purpose: Compute annualised return for irregular dated cashflows.
Tool_xirr_Info_WhyKnow: CAGR assumes one lumpsum. Real portfolios have multiple investments — XIRR is the fair comparison metric.
Tool_xirr_Info_RealLife: MF folio returns, SIP + lumpsum mix, comparing two funds with different cashflow timing.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Cagr_When: Single lumpsum held for whole period.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Sip_When: Regular monthly investments at a fixed return assumption.
Tool_xirr_Info_Alt_Mf_When: Quick lumpsum/SIP future value at assumed CAGR.
Tool_xirr_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra lets you enter 3–5 dated cashflows and solves XIRR with Newton’s method.
Tool_xirr_Info_Motto: Compare returns the way your cash actually moved.
Tool_electricity_bill_LearnText: Most state DISCOMs charge electricity in slabs — the first block of units is cheaper, higher usage costs more per unit. Enter your units and slab rates (defaults are illustrative) to estimate the bill.
Tool_electricity_bill_FormulaNote: Illustrative only. Real bills add duty, fuel surcharge, and state-specific slabs. Check your DISCOM tariff card.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab1Hint: First column: upper unit limit. Second: rate ₹/unit.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab2Hint: Second slab ends at this unit limit.
Tool_electricity_bill_Slab3Hint: All units above slab 2 at this rate.
Tool_electricity_bill_Step_3: Charge each band of units at its rate.
Tool_electricity_bill_Step_4: Add fixed charge for total bill.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Purpose: Estimate a domestic electricity bill using simple multi-slab rates.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_WhyKnow: Understanding slabs shows why cutting peak usage saves more than cutting the first cheap units.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_RealLife: Monthly household bill check, AC season planning, comparing tariff cards.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Budget_Wh: To fit the bill into overall expenses.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Inflation: To see how rising tariffs erode purchasing power.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_When: Saving the difference from lower usage.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_Exam: Redirect savings to an emergency fund.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra shows a clear per-slab breakdown plus fixed charge.
Tool_electricity_bill_Info_Motto: The last units cost the most — use slabs to your advantage.
Tool_freelancer_tax_LearnText: Freelancers and professionals can compute profit as receipts minus expenses, or opt for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA (typically 50% of gross receipts treated as income). This tool compares both and estimates tax at your marginal slab.
Tool_freelancer_tax_FormulaNote: 44ADA eligibility and turnover limits change with Budget rules. Educational estimate only — GST, advance tax, and TDS credits not modelled.
Tool_freelancer_tax_44ADAHint: When checked, taxable profit = 50% of receipts.
Tool_freelancer_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: Which method shows lower taxable profit?
Tool_freelancer_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare both before choosing a method (eligibility permitting).
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate freelancer tax under books profit or Section 44ADA.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Presumptive tax can cut compliance and sometimes tax — but only if you qualify and expenses are not very high.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_RealLife: Consultants, designers, doctors, freelancers comparing ITR methods.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: Full regime comparison with other income.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Alt_Tds_When: Clients deduct TDS on professional fees.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra puts 44ADA and books profit side-by-side with a slab tax estimate.
Tool_freelancer_tax_Info_Motto: Pick the method that fits your expenses — and the law.
Tool_gift_tax_LearnText: Under Section 56(2)(x), gifts from specified relatives are generally exempt. From non-relatives, if the aggregate gift exceeds ₹50,000 in a year, the entire amount can be taxed as income from other sources. This tool estimates tax at your slab.
Tool_gift_tax_FormulaNote: Simplified cash-gift model. Property, shares, and wedding gifts have special rules. Educational only.
Tool_gift_tax_RelativeHint: Parents, spouse, siblings, and certain in-laws are typically “specified relatives”.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Relative: Gift from a specified relative is generally fully exempt.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Taxable: Non-relative gift above the threshold — estimated tax {0} at {1}.
Tool_gift_tax_Verdict_Exempt: Non-relative gift at or below {0} — not taxable under this simplified rule.
Tool_gift_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: How relationship and amount change tax
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹2 lakh from a friend
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleStep_4: If the same gift were from a parent, tax would be ₹0.
Tool_gift_tax_ExampleStep_5: If the gift were ₹50,000 or less from a friend, tax would also be ₹0 (simplified).
Tool_gift_tax_Step_3: If non-relative and > ₹50k → full amount taxable.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Purpose: Check whether a gift is taxable and estimate tax at your slab.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Large gifts from friends or distant acquaintances can create a surprise tax liability.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_RealLife: Cash gifts, festival money from non-relatives, family transfers vs friend transfers.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To include gift income in full ITR.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Alt_80c_When: For deduction planning on other investments.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra makes the relative vs ₹50k rule obvious with a clear tax estimate.
Tool_gift_tax_Info_Motto: Know who gave the gift — it can change the tax entirely.
Tool_car_insurance_Subtitle: IDV இலிருந்து OD + மூன்றாம் தரப்பு மதிப்பீடு
Tool_car_insurance_LearnText: Own-damage (OD) premium is often quoted as a percentage of Insured Declared Value (IDV). Third-party (TP) liability is a flat IRDAI-linked amount by vehicle class. This tool multiplies IDV by your OD rate and adds a TP estimate — educational only, not an insurer quote. NCB and GST are not modelled here.
Tool_car_insurance_FormulaNote: Actual premiums depend on make/model, city, NCB, add-ons, and underwriting. Confirm with your insurer.
Tool_car_insurance_IdvHint: Approximate market value of the car for insurance.
Tool_car_insurance_OdRateHint: Typical OD rates are often around 1–3% before NCB; enter your quote rate.
Tool_car_insurance_TpHint: Flat TP liability — varies by cubic capacity; default is illustrative.
Tool_car_insurance_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower OD rate vs higher IDV
Tool_car_insurance_ExampleStep_5: NCB can cut OD further — check your renewal quote.
Tool_car_insurance_Step_2: Enter OD rate as % of IDV.
Tool_car_insurance_Step_3: Add a flat third-party premium estimate.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Purpose: Roughly estimate comprehensive premium from IDV and OD rate plus TP.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_WhyKnow: IDV and OD rate drive most of the own-damage cost; TP is largely fixed by class.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_RealLife: Comparing renewal quotes or budgeting for a new car policy.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_When: When estimating family health cover need.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Alt_Health_Examp: Sum insured by age and city tier.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra keeps OD + TP transparent from IDV and rate — no opaque quote black box.
Tool_car_insurance_Info_Motto: Know your OD math before you renew.
Tool_rent_agreement_LearnText: Rental agreements often attract stamp duty (and sometimes registration) as a percentage of total rent over the tenure. Security deposit is usually a multiple of monthly rent. This tool uses simplified state rates for education — verify with your local stamp office or lawyer.
Tool_rent_agreement_FormulaNote: Rates and whether registration is mandatory vary by state and tenure (e.g. 11 vs 12 months). Figures are illustrative.
Tool_rent_agreement_MonthsHint: Many leases use 11 months to avoid registration in some states.
Tool_rent_agreement_DepositHint: Commonly 1–6 months depending on city and landlord.
Tool_rent_agreement_ScenarioSubtitle: 11 months vs 12 months vs more deposit
Tool_rent_agreement_Step_1: Enter monthly rent and tenure months.
Tool_rent_agreement_Step_3: Set security deposit in months of rent.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Purpose: Estimate stamp/registration and security deposit for a rental lease.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_WhyKnow: Upfront cash for stamp and deposit can surprise tenants at move-in.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_RealLife: Signing a new flat lease and budgeting move-in costs.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Alt_Hra_When: For tax exemption on rent paid.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines simplified state stamp rates with deposit months in one upfront view.
Tool_rent_agreement_Info_Motto: Budget stamp and deposit before you sign.
Tool_compound_interest_LearnText: Compound interest grows principal by applying rate on accumulated interest. Frequency (annual, quarterly, monthly, daily) changes the effective return. Rule of 72 roughly estimates years to double. Same math as FinYatra’s core compound tool, under the India /calc route.
Tool_compound_interest_FormulaNote: Uses the standard compoundInterest(P, r, t, n) formula. Rates are nominal annual.
Tool_compound_interest_ScenarioSubtitle: Annual vs your frequency vs quarterly
Tool_compound_interest_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹1 lakh at 8% for 10 years
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Purpose: Project maturity with different compounding frequencies.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_WhyKnow: More frequent compounding raises effective yield for the same nominal rate.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_RealLife: Comparing FD offers or savings growth assumptions.
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Goal_2: Compare annual vs monthly vs quarterly
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Goal_3: Use Rule of 72 for doubling time
Tool_compound_interest_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For bank FD with TDS options.

Tool_compound_interest_Info_Motto: Frequency matters as much as the rate.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_LearnText: PPF allows a loan roughly from the 3rd to 6th financial year, and partial withdrawal from the 7th year onward. Official caps use balances at end of specific prior years; this tool simplifies to ~25% (loan) or ~50% (withdrawal) of your current balance for education.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_FormulaNote: Simplified fractions — your bank/PO uses prior-year closing balances. Premature closure has separate rules and penalties.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_YearHint: 1 = first FY of the account. Partial withdrawal typically from year 7.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_TooEarly: Too early — loan from year 3; partial withdrawal from year 7.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_LoanClosed: Loan facility typically ends after year 6 — use partial withdrawal instead.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Note_Mature: After maturity (15+ years), full balance may be withdrawable (extension rules apply).
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ScenarioSubtitle: Loan vs withdrawal vs later year
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_3: Loan mode at year 8 is typically closed.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_4: At year 5, loan ≈ 25% = ₹1,25,000; withdrawal not yet allowed.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_ExampleStep_5: Confirm exact limit with your bank using prior-year balances.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Step_1: Enter current account year and balance.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Step_3: Apply simplified year windows and fractions.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Purpose: Estimate how much you can borrow or partially withdraw from PPF.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_WhyKnow: Liquidity rules are year-gated; withdrawing early can be blocked or capped.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_RealLife: Planning a home down payment or education expense from PPF.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Goal_2: Know year windows for loan vs withdrawal
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra clarifies loan vs withdrawal year gates with a simple max estimate.
Tool_ppf_withdrawal_Info_Motto: Know the year gate before you need the cash.
Tool_mf_overlap_LearnText: Two equity funds can look diversified but hold the same large-cap names. Paste top holdings as symbols (optional :weight). If all holdings have weights, overlap is the sum of min(weightA, weightB); otherwise it is shared symbols ÷ union count.
Tool_mf_overlap_FormulaNote: Educational only — use latest factsheets. High overlap may mean less true diversification.
Tool_mf_overlap_HoldingsHint: Comma-separated: RELIANCE:8, TCS:6, INFY (weights optional, % of portfolio).
Tool_mf_overlap_Result_MethodFootnote: Weighted if every holding has a weight
Tool_mf_overlap_ScenarioSubtitle: Count method vs weighted vs half of Fund A
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_1: Parse both lists into symbols (and weights if given).
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_3: If weighted: sum min(wA, wB) for each shared name.
Tool_mf_overlap_ExampleStep_5: High overlap → consider different styles or categories.
Tool_mf_overlap_Step_4: Review shared names and method used.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Purpose: Measure how much two funds share the same stocks.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_WhyKnow: Owning two funds with 60%+ overlap may not diversify risk.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_RealLife: Picking a second equity fund for a SIP portfolio.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra accepts paste-friendly holdings with optional weights for a quick pairwise score.
Tool_mf_overlap_Info_Motto: Diversify holdings, not just fund names.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_LearnText: This is NOT a live government status API. It does not verify your PAN. Use it only to understand illustrative late-linking fees (historically around ₹500 / ₹1,000) and why an unlinked PAN can become inoperative for many transactions. Always check status on the Income Tax e-filing portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_FormulaNote: Penalty amounts are illustrative fixed figures for education — confirm current rules on incometax.gov.in.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_StatusHint: Self-reported only — we never call a government API.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_DeadlineHint: Historical education: lower vs higher fixed late fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Disclaimer: FinYatra does not check real PAN–Aadhaar status. Use the Income Tax Department portal to link or verify.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Ok: If already linked, no late-link fee applies in this model.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Inoper: Unlinked PAN may become inoperative — affecting TDS credit, refunds, and some high-value transactions until linked.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Consequence_Unknow: Check your status on the e-filing portal before assuming a fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower fee vs higher fee vs already linked
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleTitle: Worked example: not linked, after deadline
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleStep_2: Assume after illustrative deadline → higher fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_ExampleStep_5: Verify and link only on the official IT portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Step_3: If not linked, show fixed educational fee.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Step_4: Read inoperative-PAN consequences; verify on IT portal.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Purpose: Explain late-link fees and inoperative PAN risks without a live API.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_WhyKnow: An inoperative PAN can block refunds and complicate TDS — link early on the official site.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_RealLife: Someone unsure if they paid the late fee or need to link.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Goal_3: Know this is not a live status check
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra is explicitly educational — no fake “status API”, only fee context and consequences.
Tool_pan_aadhaar_link_Info_Motto: Link on the official portal — we only explain the stakes.

Tool_nifty_50_returns_FormulaNote: Assumed CAGR is illustrative. Past NIFTY returns do not guarantee future results.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_CagrHint: Default 12% is a common long-term equity assumption — adjust as you like.

Tool_nifty_50_returns_Step_3: Compute FV with compound / SIP formula.

Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Motto: Assume a CAGR — then stress-test it.
Tool_real_estate_LearnText: Enter property price, down payment %, loan rate and tenure, expected appreciation, and rent yield. See EMI, future property value, equity after loan paydown, and cumulative rent over the hold period.
Tool_real_estate_FormulaNote: Simplified: no taxes, maintenance, vacancy, or stamp duty. Educational only.

Tool_real_estate_Step_1: Enter price, down %, loan rate and tenure.

Tool_real_estate_Step_3: Compute EMI, FV, and loan balance after hold.
Tool_real_estate_Step_4: Read equity, rent, and illustrative net.

Tool_real_estate_Info_Alt_Home_When: When you only need EMI and interest.

Tool_lic_premium_LearnText: Enter sum assured, age, policy term, and a rough premium rate per ₹1,000 SA. The tool applies a small illustrative age loading and shows annual premium, total premiums over the term, and premium-to-cover ratio.
Tool_lic_premium_FormulaNote: Not an LIC quote. Real premiums depend on plan, health, riders, and underwriting.
Tool_lic_premium_RateHint: Example: ₹8 means ₹8 per year for every ₹1,000 of cover (before age loading).
Tool_lic_premium_ScenarioSubtitle: Same cover at lower / base / higher rate per thousand
Tool_lic_premium_ExampleStep_2: Age 30 → no age loading on base rate 8.
Tool_lic_premium_ExampleStep_5: Compare with ±2 rate scenarios for a range.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_1: Enter sum assured, age, and term.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_3: Apply illustrative age loading if age > 30.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_4: Read annual and total premium estimates.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_Purpose: Ballpark annual premium from sum assured and a rate-per-thousand assumption.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_WhyKnow: Quotes vary widely; a rough model helps size cover vs budget before talking to an agent.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_RealLife: Term vs endowment planning and 80C budgeting (plan-dependent).

Tool_lic_premium_Info_Motto: Size the cover first — then get a real quote.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Subtitle: NPS கார்பஸ் vs 50% கடைசி சம்பளம் OPS

Tool_ssy_withdrawal_LearnText: Enter SSY balance, girl's age, and account year. See max partial withdrawal for education/marriage (≈50% after age 18 and year 2), premature closure, or full maturity at 21. Educational only — confirm with Post Office.

Tool_mutual_fund_tax_LearnText: Choose equity or debt, enter buy/sell value and holding months. Equity: STCG 20% / LTCG 12.5% after ₹1.25 lakh exemption (illustrative). Debt: taxed at slab (simplified post-2023 style).

Tool_mutual_fund_tax_Step_2: Gain = sell − buy; check holding months.

Tool_mutual_fund_tax_Info_Motto: Know the gain — and the tax slice.
Tool_income_tax_refund_LearnText: Enter TDS, advance tax, self-assessment tax, and your total tax liability. See whether you get a refund or still owe tax.

Tool_income_tax_refund_Info_Alt_Tds_When: To estimate TDS on a payment.
Tool_income_tax_refund_Info_Differentiat: FinYatra nets TDS, advance, and SA against liability for a clear refund/due.
Tool_water_bill_LearnText: Municipal water is often billed in kilolitre (kl) slabs — the first block is cheaper, higher usage costs more per kl. Enter consumption and slab rates (defaults are illustrative) to estimate the bill.
Tool_water_bill_FormulaNote: Illustrative only. Real bills add sewerage, meter rent, and city-specific slabs. Check your municipal tariff card.
Tool_water_bill_Slab1Hint: First column: upper kl limit. Second: rate ₹/kl.
Tool_water_bill_Slab2Hint: Second slab ends at this kl limit.
Tool_water_bill_Slab3Hint: All kl above slab 2 at this rate.
Tool_water_bill_Step_3: Charge each band of kl at its rate.
Tool_water_bill_Step_4: Add fixed charge for total bill.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Purpose: Estimate a domestic water bill using simple multi-slab rates in kl.
Tool_water_bill_Info_WhyKnow: Understanding slabs shows why cutting high-band usage saves more than the first cheap kl.
Tool_water_bill_Info_RealLife: Monthly household bill check, summer usage planning, comparing municipal tariffs.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit the bill into overall expenses.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Inflation_When: To see how rising tariffs erode purchasing power.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_When: Saving the difference from lower usage.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Alt_Goal_Example: Redirect savings to an emergency fund.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows a clear per-slab kl breakdown plus fixed charge.
Tool_water_bill_Info_Motto: The last kilolitres cost the most — use slabs wisely.
Tool_savings_account_interest_LearnText: Banks credit savings interest on daily / average balance. Enter balance, annual rate, and days to estimate interest. Section 80TTA exempts up to ₹10,000 of savings interest for non-seniors (educational).
Tool_savings_account_interest_FormulaNot: Simplified daily product. Real banks may use min-balance or product methods. Seniors may use 80TTB (₹50,000) instead of 80TTA.
Tool_savings_account_interest_RateHint: Typical savings rates are about 2.7–3% p.a. — check your bank.
Tool_savings_account_interest_VerdictOk: Interest {0} is within the ₹10,000 Section 80TTA exemption (illustrative, non-senior).
Tool_savings_account_interest_VerdictTax: Interest {0}; about {1} may be taxable above 80TTA (illustrative).
Tool_savings_account_interest_ScenarioSu: Same balance at 2.7%, your rate, and 3%
Tool_savings_account_interest_ExampleSte: Compare 2.7% vs 3% in the scenario cards.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Purpo: Estimate interest on a savings balance and see how it relates to Section 80TTA.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_WhyKn: Idle cash earns little; knowing interest and tax exemption helps park money wisely.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_RealL: Comparing bank savings rates, parking emergency funds, tax planning for interest income.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Alt_F: When you can lock money for higher rates.

Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Diffe: FinYatra pairs simple interest math with a clear 80TTA exemption note.
Tool_savings_account_interest_Info_Motto: Know what your idle cash earns — and what 80TTA covers.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_LearnText: Sovereign Gold Bonds pay 2.5% p.a. on the issue price for 8 years (paid semi-annually). At redemption you get the prevailing gold price. Capital gains on maturity for individuals are typically tax-free — this tool is educational.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_FormulaNote: Illustrative. Issue price and redemption price vary by tranche. Interest is taxable; maturity capital gains for individuals are generally exempt.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_YearsHint: SGB tenure is 8 years (exit window from year 5 on interest payment dates).
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_RedeemHint: Use an assumed future gold price to see capital gains education.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_VerdictNote: Invested {0}; interest {1}; capital gain {2}. Maturity gains for individuals are generally tax-free (educational).
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_ScenarioSubtitl: Flat gold vs your assumption vs +20%
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Step_2: Set tenure (default 8) and assumed redemption price.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Purpose: Estimate interest and capital gains on Sovereign Gold Bonds.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_WhyKnow: SGB combines gold price exposure with a fixed 2.5% coupon and favourable tax on maturity.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_RealLife: Comparing SGB vs physical gold or gold ETF for long-term holding.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Alt_Gold_W: For digital/physical gold without SGB coupon.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Alt_Fd_Whe: For fixed rupee returns without gold risk.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows SGB coupon plus redemption gain scenarios in one view.
Tool_sovereign_gold_bond_Info_Motto: Gold price plus 2.5% — know both sides of SGB.

Tool_senior_citizen_fd_LearnText: Banks often offer ~0.5% extra on senior citizen FDs. Enter deposit, senior rate, and tenure to see maturity. TDS applies if interest exceeds ₹50,000 for seniors (illustrative).

Tool_senior_citizen_fd_VerdictTds: Net maturity {0} after estimated TDS of {1}.

Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Step_4: Apply TDS note if above threshold.

Tool_senior_citizen_fd_Info_WhyKnow: The senior rate bump compounds meaningfully over multi-year tenures.

Tool_nri_tax_Subtitle: குடியிருப்பு நிலை + இந்திய வருமானம் → மதிப்பிடப்பட்ட வரி

Tool_nri_tax_VerdictNri: NRI/RNOR: typically India-sourced income is taxed in India; foreign income usually is not (subject to exceptions / DTAA).

Tool_nri_tax_Info_Motto: Status first — then tax the right income.

Tool_post_office_savings_Info_WhyKnow: Helps compare POSA-style returns with bank savings or TD.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_RealLife: Small deposits parked in Post Office savings.
Tool_post_office_savings_Info_Differenti: FinYatra models PO savings compounding next to TD, MIS, and PPF tools.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_LearnText: Enter basic pay, DA%, HRA%, other allowances, and total deductions. See DA, HRA, gross, and net — a simplified central government salary slip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_FormulaNot: Illustrative. Actual slips include NPS, CGEGIS, income tax, and city-class HRA — confirm with your DDO / payslip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_HraHint: Often 30% / 20% / 10% by city class — enter your rate.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Deductions: NPS, CGEGIS, income tax, and other recoveries combined.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_ExampleSte: Gross = basic + DA + HRA + other allowances.
A DA hike raises both gross and net if deductions stay fixed.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Step_4: Gross = sum of earnings; net = gross − deductions.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Abbre: Central govt payslip — basic + DA + HRA
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Purpo: Estimate gross and net from a simplified 7th CPC-style slip.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_WhyKn: DA revisions and HRA city class change take-home quickly.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_RealL: Central government employees checking monthly in-hand.
Tool_central_govt_salary_slip_Info_Diffe: FinYatra builds a central-govt style slip with DA and HRA percentages in one view.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_LearnText: Enter one annual deposit (capped at ₹1.5 lakh) and rates. Compare SSY (15 deposit years, mature at 21) with PPF (15 years) side by side.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: SSY is for a girl child; PPF is open to individuals. Rates change quarterly — educational comparison only.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_DepositHint: Max ₹1.5 lakh per year for both schemes (shared cap in this tool).
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_2: SSY: deposit 15 years, interest continues to year 21 at 8.2%.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_3: PPF: 15 years at 7.1% with annual compounding.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_4: Gap = SSY maturity − PPF maturity (often favours SSY on rate + time).
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: Eligibility differs — SSY needs a girl child account.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Step_1: Enter the same annual deposit for both.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Step_3: Project SSY to year 21 and PPF to year 15.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Compare Sukanya Samriddhi and PPF for the same yearly deposit.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Both are EEE small-savings favourites — rate and tenure differ.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Parents choosing between SSY and PPF for long-term goals.
Tool_ssy_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra puts SSY and PPF maturity on one screen for the same deposit.
Tool_pension_commutation_LearnText: Commutation lets you take a portion of pension as a one-time lump sum. Enter monthly pension, commutation % (max 40%), and a commutation factor (years of purchase). The tool shows lump sum, reduced monthly pension, and a simple break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_FormulaNote: Simplified educational model. Official factors vary by age and service rules; restoration after ~15 years is not modelled here.
Tool_pension_commutation_PercentHint: Typically up to 40% of pension can be commuted.
Tool_pension_commutation_FactorHint: Years of purchase — ~9.81 is a common illustrative age-60 factor.
Tool_pension_commutation_Step_4: See lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Abbreviati: Pension commutation — lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Purpose: Estimate lump sum and reduced pension when commuting a share of pension.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_WhyKnow: Commutation trades lifelong pension for cash now — break-even helps decide.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_RealLife: Central/state pensioners choosing commutation at retirement.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Goal_2: See lump sum vs reduced pension
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Nps_Wh: For contribution and corpus planning before annuity.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Annuit: For pension from a retirement corpus.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Alt_Tax_Wh: For tax on pension / lump sum context.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows lump sum, reduced pension, and break-even in one view.
Tool_pension_commutation_Info_Motto: Cash now vs pension forever — know the trade-off.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_LearnText: Under the old regime, Section 24(b) allows interest deduction (₹2L cap if self-occupied) and Section 80C allows principal repayment up to ₹1.5L. Enter first-year interest and principal, occupancy, regime, and slab to estimate tax saving.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_FormulaNote: Educational. New regime generally disallows these deductions. Joint-loan higher limits are not auto-modelled — enter your share of interest/principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_RegimeHint: New regime: 24(b)/80C home-loan benefits typically not available (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ScenarioSubti: Self-occupied vs let-out vs new regime
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_ExampleStep_1: Interest ₹2,50,000; principal ₹1,80,000; self-occupied; old regime; 30% slab.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Purpose: Estimate old-regime tax saving from home-loan interest and principal.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_WhyKnow: Self-occupied interest is capped; let-out interest is often fully deductible (simplified).
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_RealLife: First home loan, comparing old vs new regime, joint ownership share.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Home: To derive EMI and first-year split from loan terms.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_80c_: To plan the full ₹1.5L basket including ELSS/PPF.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Tax_: For overall old vs new regime compare.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Differen: FinYatra isolates 24(b) and 80C with old vs new regime contrast.
Tool_home_loan_tax_benefit_Info_Motto: Interest and principal — two deductions, one home loan.
Tool_ltcg_LearnText: Long-term capital gains (LTCG) apply after the holding threshold — 12 months for equity, 24 months for property (simplified). Equity gets a ₹1.25L exemption then 12.5%; property is taxed at 12.5% with optional 54/54EC exemption.
Tool_ltcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative post-Budget rates. Indexation for property is not auto-applied here — enter indexed cost as buy price if needed.
Tool_ltcg_ExemptionHint: Optional amount that reduces taxable LTCG.
Tool_ltcg_Result_BelowThreshold: Holding below LTCG threshold — LTCG math shown for reference
Tool_ltcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, and holding months.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate long-term capital gains tax on equity or property.
Tool_ltcg_Info_WhyKnow: Holding period and the ₹1.25L equity exemption change the tax bill.
Tool_ltcg_Info_RealLife: Selling stocks after a year, property sale with 54EC bonds.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: When you need STCG and LTCG in one tool.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For tax-saving equity with LTCG on exit.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on LTCG with equity exemption and 54/54EC inputs.
Tool_ltcg_Info_Motto: Hold long enough — then tax the gain, not the whole sale.
Tool_stcg_LearnText: Short-term capital gains (STCG) apply before the long-term threshold. Listed equity STCG is often 20%; property STCG is taxed at your slab. Compare selling now vs waiting for LTCG.
Tool_stcg_FormulaNote: Illustrative rates. Waiting assumes the same sell price — markets can move.
Tool_stcg_HoldingHint: STCG if equity < 12 months or property < 24 months.
Tool_stcg_ScenarioSubtitle: STCG today vs LTCG after threshold
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_4: If waited for LTCG: exempt ₹1.25L, taxable ₹25,000 × 12.5%.
Tool_stcg_ExampleStep_5: Compare tax saved by waiting (price assumed unchanged).
Tool_stcg_Step_2: Enter buy, sell, holding, and slab.
Tool_stcg_Step_3: See STCG tax at 20% or slab.
Tool_stcg_Info_Purpose: Estimate short-term capital gains tax and wait-vs-sell impact.
Tool_stcg_Info_WhyKnow: A few months of holding can change equity tax from 20% STCG to 12.5% LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_RealLife: Booking profits early, property flip, year-end tax planning.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Cg_When: For both STCG and LTCG together.
Tool_stcg_Info_Alt_Elss_When: For lock-in equity that exits as LTCG.
Tool_stcg_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra highlights sell-now STCG vs wait-for-LTCG side by side.
Tool_stcg_Info_Motto: Sometimes waiting a few months is the cheapest tax tip.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_LearnText: Tax-loss harvesting uses realised losses to offset gains. Simplified India rules: STCL can set off STCG then LTCG; LTCL can set off only LTCG. Unused losses may be carried forward (shown as remaining).
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_FormulaNote: Educational set-off order. India has no US-style wash-sale rule, but other conditions apply. Confirm with a CA.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_1: STCL ₹1.5L offsets STCG ₹2L → net STCG ₹50k; STCL left = 0.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_ExampleStep_4: Compare with tax if no losses were booked.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Step_4: See net gains and tax saved.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Purpose: Estimate how realised losses reduce taxable capital gains.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_WhyKnow: Year-end harvesting can cut tax without changing long-term allocation much.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_RealLife: Booking losers before 31 Mar, rebalancing with tax awareness.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Goal_3: See tax saved vs no harvest
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Differenti: FinYatra applies simplified STCL/LTCL set-off and shows tax saved clearly.
Tool_tax_loss_harvesting_Info_Motto: Harvest losses with rules — not randomly.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_LearnText: Advance tax is paid in instalments: 15% by 15 Jun, 45% by 15 Sep, 75% by 15 Dec, and 100% by 15 Mar. Enter estimated tax and amount already paid to see remaining tax, shortfall for the current milestone, and the full calendar.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_FormulaNote: Educational schedule. 234C interest on shortfall is not computed here — use the interest/penalty tool for that. Presumptive taxpayers may have different timing.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_PaidHint: Advance tax + TDS credited toward liability (simplified).
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_CalendarSubtit: Cumulative % and amount due by each date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_ExampleStep_5: Use the calendar for Jun/Sep/Dec/Mar milestones.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Step_1: Enter estimated tax and amount paid.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Abbreviat: Advance tax due dates — quarterly calendar
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Purpose: Show how much advance tax is due by each quarterly date.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_WhyKnow: Missing a milestone can attract 234C interest.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_RealLife: Self-employed quarterly payments, freelancers tracking TDS + advance.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_1: Enter estimated tax and paid amount
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Goal_2: See shortfall for current due date
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Tds_W: When TDS already covers part of tax.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Alt_Advan: For installment amount focus with same schedule.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Different: FinYatra centres the due-date calendar with remaining-to-milestone amounts.
Tool_advance_tax_due_date_Info_Motto: Know the date — then pay the percent.
Tool_ups_LearnText: Enter basic pay, service years, and contribution rates. See UPS assured pension (50% at 25 years, pro-rata below) versus a simplified NPS corpus/pension from the same contributions.
Tool_ups_FormulaNote: Educational only. Official UPS rules, DA, family pension, and NPS annuity options differ — confirm with your cadre / CA.
Tool_ups_YearsHint: Full 50% assured pension typically needs 25 years (simplified).
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_3: Monthly contrib = 80,000 × 28.5% = ₹22,800 invested in NPS comparison.
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_4: NPS corpus grows for 25 years; pension ≈ corpus × annuity%/12.
Tool_ups_ExampleStep_5: Gap = UPS pension − NPS pension (educational comparison only).
Tool_ups_Step_1: Enter monthly basic and years of service.
Tool_ups_Step_4: Compare with NPS SIP corpus → annuity pension.
Tool_ups_Info_Purpose: Estimate assured UPS pension and compare with a simplified NPS path.
Tool_ups_Info_WhyKnow: Central govt employees weighing UPS vs NPS need a clear pension gap view.
Tool_ups_Info_RealLife: Choosing or understanding UPS assured payout versus market-linked NPS.
Tool_ups_Info_Alt_Nps_When: For full Tier-I corpus and annuity split.
Tool_ups_Info_Alt_Epf_When: For EPF corpus, not pension scheme choice.
Tool_ups_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows UPS assured % vs a same-contribution NPS estimate in one view.
Tool_ups_Info_Motto: Assured vs market — see the gap.
Tool_itr_form_LearnText: Tick the income sources that apply. We suggest ITR-1, 2, 3, or 4 using simplified rules — always confirm on the Income Tax portal before filing.
Tool_itr_form_FormulaNote: Educational selector only. Defective returns, schedules, and edge cases need a CA or the official utility.
Tool_itr_form_ChecklistHint: Select all that apply to your FY income.
Tool_itr_form_ExampleStep_2: No capital gains, business, or foreign assets.
Tool_itr_form_ExampleStep_5: Adding capital gains would move you to ITR-2.
Tool_itr_form_Step_1: Tick all income sources that apply.
Tool_itr_form_Info_Purpose: Suggest which ITR form may fit your income mix.
Tool_itr_form_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong form can lead to defective return notices from CPC.
Tool_itr_form_Info_RealLife: Salaried filers checking Sahaj vs ITR-2 before e-filing.
Tool_itr_form_Info_Goal_2: See why that form was chosen
Tool_itr_form_Info_Goal_3: Spot what would change the form
Tool_itr_form_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To estimate tax liability, not form type.
Tool_itr_form_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra turns a source checklist into a plain-language ITR form hint.
Tool_gst_itc_LearnText: Enter outward GST liability and eligible ITC. See net cash payable or leftover credit — simplified, not GSTR-2B / Rule 42 reconciliation.
Tool_gst_itc_FormulaNote: Blocked credits, reversals, and portal matching are not modelled. Confirm with a GST practitioner.
Tool_gst_itc_ItcHint: Eligible credit you can set off against outward tax.
Tool_gst_itc_ExampleStep_4: If ITC were higher than outward, excess credit remains.
Tool_gst_itc_ExampleStep_5: Educational only — not a substitute for GSTR-3B.
Tool_gst_itc_Info_Abbreviation: GST ITC — net payable after credit
Tool_gst_itc_Info_Purpose: Quick net GST cash after setting off ITC.
Tool_gst_itc_Info_WhyKnow: Helps plan working capital before monthly GST payment.
Tool_gst_itc_Info_RealLife: Traders estimating 3B cash after invoice ITC.
Tool_gst_itc_Info_Alt_Freelancer_When: When tracking income tax on receipts.
Tool_gst_itc_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows outward − ITC net payable without portal complexity.
Tool_esi_LearnText: Enter monthly wages. If within the illustrative ₹21,000 ceiling, see employee and employer ESI contributions. Above ceiling, typically not covered (simplified).
Tool_esi_FormulaNote: Rates and ceiling can change. Benefits value and state rules are not modelled — educational only.
Tool_esi_ExampleStep_5: If wages exceed the ceiling, this simplified tool shows not eligible.
Tool_esi_Info_Purpose: Estimate monthly ESI employee and employer shares.
Tool_esi_Info_WhyKnow: Payroll and take-home planning for covered employees.
Tool_esi_Info_RealLife: HR / employees checking ESI on a wage slip.
Tool_esi_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows 0.75% + 3.25% with a clear wage-ceiling note.
Tool_dividend_tax_LearnText: Enter dividend income and your marginal slab. See tax with 4% cess, illustrative TDS if over ₹10k, and a surcharge band note from total income.
Tool_dividend_tax_FormulaNote: DDT abolished. Surcharge on total income is simplified. DTAA / Sec 57 interest deduction not modelled.
Tool_dividend_tax_TotalIncomeHint: Used only for surcharge band note (simplified).
Tool_dividend_tax_ExampleStep_4: No DDT — tax is in your hands at slab.
Tool_dividend_tax_ExampleStep_5: Surcharge band depends on total income, not dividend alone.
Tool_dividend_tax_Step_4: Read surcharge band from total income.
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_Abbreviation: Dividend tax — slab + cess, no DDT
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate tax on equity/MF dividends at your slab.
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Dividends are taxable in your hands after DDT abolition.
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_RealLife: Investors planning dividend vs growth MF tax.
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows slab tax on dividends with TDS and surcharge notes in one place.
Tool_dividend_tax_Info_Motto: No DDT — tax at your slab.
Tool_nps_tier_2_LearnText: Enter monthly Tier-II contribution, expected return, and years. See projected corpus, invested amount, and gains. Tier-II is voluntary and withdrawable unlike Tier-I.
Tool_nps_tier_2_FormulaNote: Tax treatment of Tier-II gains depends on scheme and holding — educational projection only. Expense ratio not modelled.
Tool_nps_tier_2_ExistingHint: Optional opening balance already in Tier-II.
Tool_nps_tier_2_ExampleStep_3: Corpus = SIP future value at 10% p.a.
Tool_nps_tier_2_ExampleStep_5: Tier-II stays liquid — no Tier-I lock-in in this model.
Tool_nps_tier_2_Info_Purpose: Project Tier-II corpus with SIP-style contributions.
Tool_nps_tier_2_Info_WhyKnow: Tier-II offers low-cost market exposure with liquidity vs Tier-I lock-in.
Tool_nps_tier_2_Info_RealLife: NPS subscribers parking surplus in Tier-II.
Tool_nps_tier_2_Info_Alt_Nps_When: For Tier-I retirement corpus and annuity.
Tool_nps_tier_2_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra projects liquid Tier-II corpus separately from locked Tier-I tools.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Subtitle: முதல் + விகிதம் + ஆண்டுகள் → ஆண்டு வளர்ச்சி & விதி 72
Tool_power_of_compounding_LearnText: Enter principal, annual rate, and years. See maturity, total gain, years to double (Rule of 72), and a year-by-year growth table. Compare lower vs higher rates.
Tool_power_of_compounding_FormulaNote: Annual compounding. Rule of 72 is an approximation — real doubling time varies with rate and frequency.
Tool_power_of_compounding_ScenarioSubtit: Lower vs base vs higher return
Tool_power_of_compounding_ExampleStep_4: Rule of 72: 72 ÷ 12 = 6 years to roughly double.
Tool_power_of_compounding_ExampleStep_5: Yearly table shows balance after each year.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Step_4: Read maturity, Rule of 72, and yearly table.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Purpose: See how principal grows year by year and when it roughly doubles.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_WhyKnow: Small rate differences compound into large gaps over long horizons.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_RealLife: Comparing PPF vs equity vs aggressive equity over 10–20 years.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Goal_1: Project maturity from P, r, n
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Alt_Sip_W: For monthly investments instead of a lump sum.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Alt_Fd_Wh: For bank FD compounding and TDS.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Alt_Ppf_W: For PPF annual deposits and 15-year lock-in.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Different: FinYatra pairs a yearly growth table with Rule of 72 in one educational view.
Tool_power_of_compounding_Info_Motto: Time and rate turn small starts into large outcomes.
Tool_crop_insurance_LearnText: Enter sum insured and season (or custom rate). See farmer premium and an educational note that claims are capped around sum insured — not a claim filing tool.
Tool_crop_insurance_FormulaNote: Illustrative PMFBY farmer shares. Actual premium, subsidy, and claim settlement follow scheme notifications.
Tool_crop_insurance_RateHint: Defaults to typical PMFBY farmer share for the season.
Tool_crop_insurance_Result_ClaimFootnote: Educational: up to sum insured if total loss — confirm with insurer
Tool_crop_insurance_ScenarioSubtitle: Rabi vs your inputs vs commercial
Tool_crop_insurance_ExampleStep_4: Claim note shows up to ₹2,00,000 for total loss (illustrative).
Tool_crop_insurance_ExampleStep_5: Confirm actual PMFBY rates and claim process with your bank/insurer.
Tool_crop_insurance_Step_1: Enter sum insured for the crop.
Tool_crop_insurance_Step_2: Choose season or set custom rate.
Tool_crop_insurance_Info_Purpose: Estimate farmer share of crop insurance premium from sum insured and rate.
Tool_crop_insurance_Info_WhyKnow: Knowing the premium helps budget before sowing season.
Tool_crop_insurance_Info_RealLife: Farmers enrolling under PMFBY via bank or CSC.

Tool_crop_insurance_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows PMFBY-style farmer premium and a clear educational claim note.
Tool_crop_insurance_Info_Motto: Budget the premium before you sow.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_LearnText: Select an eligibility category and family size. If eligible, the tool displays the illustrative ₹5 lakh/family/year PM-JAY cover and a gap vs any private cover you already have. Not an official eligibility API.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_FormulaNote: Educational display only. Confirm eligibility on the official PM-JAY / state portal.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_EligHint: Self-declared category for education — verify on the official portal.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_Result_GapFootnote: Cover still useful after existing private cover
Tool_ayushman_bharat_ScenarioSubtitle: SECC vs senior 70+ vs not listed
Tool_ayushman_bharat_ExampleStep_1: Family size = 4; eligibility = SECC; existing cover = ₹0.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_ExampleStep_4: If not listed, cover shows ₹0 in this educational tool.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_ExampleStep_5: Always verify on the official PM-JAY portal.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_Step_4: See ₹5L cover display and gap note.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_Info_Purpose: Show illustrative ₹5 lakh family cover when eligibility category suggests PM-JAY.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_Info_WhyKnow: Knowing the cover amount helps plan hospitalisation risk alongside private insurance.
Tool_ayushman_bharat_Info_RealLife: Families checking whether PM-JAY may apply before buying private cover.

Tool_ayushman_bharat_Info_Motto: Know the ₹5L cover — then verify eligibility officially.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Subtitle: அதே வீட்டுச் செலவு → HRA vs கடன் வரி பலன்
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_LearnText: Enter annual housing cost (rent or EMI proxy), basic, HRA received, metro status, interest share of loan cost, and tax slab. Compare HRA exemption tax saving vs Section 24(b)+80C home-loan tax saving (old regime, educational).
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_FormulaNote: Old regime only. Loan path splits housing cost into interest/principal by your share %. Not a full rent-vs-buy NPV model.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_HousingHint: Use annual rent paid, or approximate annual EMI (interest + principal).
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_InterestHint: Of housing cost treated as interest for 24(b); rest as principal for 80C.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Result_WinnerLoan: Home loan path saves more tax
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ScenarioSubtitle: Non-metro vs base vs lower slab
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ExampleStep_1: Housing cost = ₹3,60,000; basic = ₹6,00,000; HRA = ₹2,40,000; metro; slab 30%.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ExampleStep_2: HRA exemption = min of three Rule 2A amounts; tax saving = exemption × 30%.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ExampleStep_3: Loan: interest = 70% of cost (capped ₹2L for 24(b)); principal share for 80C.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ExampleStep_4: Compare both tax savings and the advantage gap.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_ExampleStep_5: Decision also depends on ownership, EMI, and new vs old regime — not modelled fully here.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Step_1: Enter housing cost, basic, and HRA.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Step_3: Set interest share for the loan path.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_Purpose: Compare tax benefit of renting with HRA vs buying with a home loan for similar annual housing cost.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_WhyKnow: Tax alone rarely decides rent vs buy — but it is a useful first filter.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_RealLife: Salaried employees choosing between continuing rent and taking a home loan.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_Goal_3: See which path wins on tax
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_Alt_Tax_When: For full old vs new regime tax.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra compares HRA and home-loan tax benefits on the same housing-cost base.
Tool_hra_vs_home_loan_Info_Motto: Compare the tax side of rent vs buy on equal footing.
Tool_employee_compensation_Subtitle: CTC கூறுகள் → முதலாளி செலவு vs இன்-ஹேண்ட்
Tool_employee_compensation_LearnText: Enter CTC, basic %, PF basis, optional ESI, and rough annual tax/PT deductions. See employer total cost (CTC + extras) versus employee monthly in-hand — rough educational model.
Tool_employee_compensation_FormulaNote: Simplified CTC split. Real offers vary by structure, variable pay, and state PT/ESI rules.
Tool_employee_compensation_DeductionsHin: Rough income tax and professional tax to subtract for in-hand.
