Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_Alt_Emi_When: When loan amount is already fixed.
Tool_nri_home_loan_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines NRI-style rates with FOIR eligibility and EMI.
Tool_property_capital_gains_LearnText: For property, holding 24+ months is typically long-term. Enter buy and sell prices, holding months, and any Sec 54/54EC exemption to estimate taxable gain and tax at the simplified LTCG rate.
Tool_property_capital_gains_FormulaNote: Post-Budget simplified 12.5% without indexation for many cases. Indexation choice and inherited-property rules need a CA — educational only.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Result_Below: Below 24 months — shown as LTCG path for reference
Tool_property_capital_gains_ScenarioSubt: No exemption vs your input vs higher exemption
Tool_property_capital_gains_ExampleStep_: Try exemption scenarios to see tax drop.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Purpose: Estimate long-term capital gains tax on property sale.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_WhyKnow: Property LTCG and exemptions drive net sale proceeds.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_RealLif: Selling a house or plot and planning Sec 54 reinvestment.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Alt_Cg_: For equity, gold, or debt MF too.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Alt_Hom: For rental NAV, not sale CG.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Differe: FinYatra wraps property LTCG with optional 54/54EC in one flow.
Tool_property_capital_gains_Info_Motto: Sale price is not take-home — check LTCG first.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_LearnText: Form 26AS shows TDS credited against your PAN. Enter TDS from 26AS and from your books (or Form 16) to see if they match and by how much they differ.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_FormulaNot: Also cross-check AIS/TIS. Missing credit may need deductor follow-up. Educational reconciliation only.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_BooksHint: Sum of TDS from salary, interest, contractors, etc. in your records.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_VerdictMat: 26AS and books match — good to proceed with ITR credit checks.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Verdict26a: 26AS shows {0} more than books — verify if books under-recorded TDS.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_VerdictBoo: Books show {0} more than 26AS — follow up with deductors / AIS for missing credit.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_ScenarioSu: Perfect match vs your figures vs books higher
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_ExampleSte: Check if books missed a Form 16A / interest TDS.
Align books or claim the higher 26AS credit carefully.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Purpo: Spot gaps between Form 26AS credits and your books.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_WhyKn: ITR refunds and demand notices often start with TDS mismatches.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_RealL: Year-end ITR prep and AIS vs Form 16 checks.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Alt_T: To estimate TDS on a payment.
Tool_form_26as_reconciliation_Info_Diffe: FinYatra shows 26AS vs books difference in one glance.
Tool_e_shram_LearnText: e-Shram registers unorganised workers and links them to social-security schemes. Toggle registration and schemes to see illustrative annual premiums, accident/life cover, and PM-SYM pension note.
Tool_e_shram_FormulaNote: Premiums and covers are illustrative and change by scheme year. Not an enrolment tool — use official portals.
Tool_e_shram_RegisteredHint: Benefits shown only when registered is Yes.
Tool_e_shram_VerdictNo: Register on e-Shram to unlock linked social-security pathways (educational).
Tool_e_shram_ScenarioSubtitle: Not registered vs your picks vs full suite
Tool_e_shram_ExampleStep_1: Registered = Yes; all three schemes on.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Purpose: Educational snapshot of schemes linked to e-Shram registration.
Tool_e_shram_Info_WhyKnow: Unorganised workers can access low-cost life, accident, and pension schemes.
Tool_e_shram_Info_RealLife: Gig, construction, and informal-sector workers checking benefits.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit premiums into cash flow.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Alt_Term_When: For larger life cover than PMJJBY.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra summarises e-Shram-linked scheme premiums and covers together.
Tool_e_shram_Info_Motto: Register first — then pick the schemes that fit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_LearnText: EPF often includes an employer share and a higher rate; PPF is voluntary with a ₹1.5L annual cap. Enter the same monthly contribution and years to compare maturity side by side.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: EPF employee 12% implies basic = contribution / 0.12 when employer match is on. PPF capped at ₹1.5L/year. Educational projection.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_MonthlyHint: Employee EPF share (12% of basic) or PPF monthly equivalent.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ScenarioSubtitle: No employer match vs base vs longer tenure
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_2: EPF with employer 3.67% at ~8.25% for 15 years.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: Toggle employer match off to see employee-only EPF.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Side-by-side maturity for the same contribution habit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Employer match and rate differences can dominate long-term corpus.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Salaried savers choosing extra PPF vs relying on EPF.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra compares EPF (with optional employer match) against PPF on the same monthly habit.
Tool_epf_vs_ppf_Info_Motto: Same rupee in — see which corpus wins.
Tool_startup_tax_LearnText: Enter business profit, DPIIT eligibility, and your slab. Eligible DPIIT startups get an illustrative 100% 80-IAC deduction for the year; otherwise tax is profit × slab% + 4% cess. Angel tax on share premium is noted as abolished for DPIIT startups.
Tool_startup_tax_FormulaNote: Educational one-year view. Real 80-IAC has year limits, incorporation conditions, and turnover caps — confirm with your CA.
Tool_startup_tax_DpiitHint: Simplified: if Yes, 100% of profit is deducted under 80-IAC for this year.
Tool_startup_tax_Result_SavedFootnote: Compared with same profit at your slab
Tool_startup_tax_Angel_Abolished: For DPIIT-recognised startups, angel tax on share premium is treated as abolished in this educational view.
Tool_startup_tax_Angel_Na: Without DPIIT recognition, angel-tax rules may still apply on certain share issues — this tool does not compute angel tax.
Tool_startup_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: No DPIIT vs your inputs vs half profit
Tool_startup_tax_ExampleSubtitle: 80-IAC → zero tax in this simplified year
Tool_startup_tax_ExampleStep_5: Angel tax note: abolished for DPIIT in this model.
Tool_startup_tax_Step_3: Apply 100% deduction or tax on profit.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate tax on startup profit with an illustrative DPIIT 80-IAC deduction.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_WhyKnow: DPIIT recognition can wipe out tax on eligible profits for limited years.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_RealLife: Planning tax for a DPIIT-recognised startup’s first profitable years.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Goal_2: Note angel tax status for DPIIT
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra pairs DPIIT 80-IAC with a simple profit tax and angel-tax note.
Tool_startup_tax_Info_Motto: Know what DPIIT recognition can save before you plan the year.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_LearnText: Enter last drawn Basic+DA for gratuity, monthly basic for EPF, years of service, and EPF rate. Compare Act-style gratuity with a projected EPF balance — educational, not a retirement plan.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_FormulaNote: Gratuity uses (Basic+DA)×15×years/26. EPF compounds employee+employer EPF share. EPS is not added to corpus.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_SalaryHint: Used in the Payment of Gratuity Act formula.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_BasicHint: PF wages for contribution projection (on actual basic).
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ScenarioSubtitle: Shorter tenure vs base vs +5 years
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ExampleStep_3: EPF grows monthly contributions over 20 years to a much larger corpus.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_ExampleStep_5: Both are retirement benefits — compare, don’t substitute one for the other.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Purpose: Compare projected gratuity lump sum with EPF corpus for the same service years.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_WhyKnow: They grow differently — linear formula vs compounding contributions.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_RealLife: Planning exit benefits after long service in a covered establishment.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Goal_1: See gratuity vs EPF side by side
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Goal_2: Understand the gap as tenure grows
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Alt_Gratuity_W: Only gratuity amount and tax exemption.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra shows gratuity and EPF for the same tenure in one view.
Tool_gratuity_vs_epf_Info_Motto: See both exit benefits before you plan retirement cash.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_LearnText: Enter interest income, other IFOS amounts, and your slab. Optionally apply 80TTA (₹10k) or 80TTB (₹50k seniors) on interest. Tax ≈ taxable IFOS × slab% + 4% cess.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_FormulaNo: Simplified IFOS view. Lottery/winnings rates, gift exemptions, and family-pension deductions are not fully modelled.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_OtherHint: Taxable gifts, family pension, etc. (simplified total).
Tool_income_from_other_sources_ScenarioS: No deduction vs base vs higher slab
Tool_income_from_other_sources_ExampleSt: Seniors can use 80TTB up to ₹50,000 instead.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Purp: Estimate tax on interest and other IFOS at your slab.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_WhyK: IFOS is often overlooked until TDS or ITR time.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Real: FD interest, dividends already taxed elsewhere, taxable gifts, family pension.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Diff: FinYatra taxes interest + other IFOS at slab with optional 80TTA/B.
Tool_income_from_other_sources_Info_Mott: Price your other income before ITR season.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_LearnText: Enter the same annual contribution (capped at PPF ₹1.5L), years, NPS expected return, and PPF rate. See side-by-side maturity and the gap. NPS is market-linked; PPF is government-backed — educational only.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_FormulaNote: NPS uses monthly SIP FV; PPF uses annual compounding. Annuity lock-in and tax differences are not fully modelled.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_AnnualHint: Capped at ₹1,50,000 for a fair PPF compare.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ScenarioSubtitle: Half contribution vs base vs +5 years
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_4: Gap = NPS − PPF (usually favours NPS at higher return).
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_ExampleStep_5: PPF is EEE and locked; NPS has annuity rules — compare risk too.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Purpose: Compare maturity for the same annual outlay in NPS and PPF.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_WhyKnow: Higher NPS return comes with market risk; PPF is steadier.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_RealLife: Choosing how to split ₹1.5L between PPF and NPS.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Goal_3: Test half contribution or longer tenure
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra compares the same annual rupee in NPS and PPF side by side.
Tool_nps_vs_ppf_Info_Motto: Match the same outlay before you pick the vehicle.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_LearnText: Enter the foreign remittance under LRS. This simplified model uses 5% if amount ≤ ₹7 lakh and 20% if above. Education/medical exceptions and Budget rate changes are not fully modelled — confirm with your bank.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_FormulaNo: Illustrative LRS TCS. Thresholds and purpose-based rates change — verify with authorised dealer / CA.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_AmountHin: Total remittance under Liberalised Remittance Scheme.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Status_No: Enter a remittance amount to see TCS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_ScenarioS: At/below ₹7L vs your amount vs higher
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_ExampleSt: TCS is usually adjustable as tax credit in ITR.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Step_4: See TCS and net cash needed.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Abbr: TCS — Tax collected on LRS remittance
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Purp: Estimate TCS when sending money abroad under LRS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_WhyK: Banks collect TCS upfront — it affects how much cash you need.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Real: Foreign education fees, travel, or overseas investments under LRS.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Alt_: Tax deducted by payer, not collected on remittance.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Diff: FinYatra focuses LRS remittance on the 5%/20% TCS split.
Tool_tcs_on_foreign_remittance_Info_Mott: Budget the TCS before you remit abroad.
Tool_rental_yield_LearnText: Enter property value, monthly rent, and annual expenses (tax, maintenance, vacancy). See gross yield (rent/value) and net yield after expenses. Typical city yields often sit around 2–5% — educational only.
Tool_rental_yield_FormulaNote: Does not model appreciation, leverage, or house-property tax. Compare with other assets carefully.
Tool_rental_yield_ExpensesHint: Property tax, maintenance, insurance, vacancy allowance.
Tool_rental_yield_ExampleStep_5: Compare with FD/equity yields and tax on rental income separately.
Tool_rental_yield_Step_2: Enter monthly rent and annual expenses.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Abbreviation: Rental yield — income return on property
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Purpose: Measure gross and net rental yield on an investment property.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_WhyKnow: Yield shows income return separate from price appreciation.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_RealLife: Comparing a rental flat with FD or equity MF income.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Alt_Fd_When: Compare income yield with bank FD.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows gross and net rental yield from rent, value, and expenses.
Tool_rental_yield_Info_Motto: Know the income yield before you buy for rent.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_LearnText: Enter Basic+DA, employee and employer NPS, other 80C used, and your slab. Old regime unlocks 80CCD(1) within ₹1.5L and extra ₹50K under (1B). Employer NPS under 80CCD(2) works in both regimes (10% private / 14% central).
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_FormulaNote: Educational. Confirm limits and regime rules with your CA. Cess 4% applied on tax saved.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Other80cHint: PPF, ELSS, life insurance, etc. — reduces room for 80CCD(1).
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_ScenarioSubti: New regime vs your inputs vs higher employer NPS
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_WhyKnow: Employer NPS and the extra ₹50K (1B) are easy to miss when planning tax.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_RealLife: Optimising salary-structure NPS and personal Tier-I contributions.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_Goal_3: Estimate tax saved at your slab
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_Alt_Nps_: Corpus and pension projection, not tax limits.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_Differen: FinYatra shows 80CCD(1)/(1B)/(2) caps and tax saved side by side.
Tool_80ccd_nps_tax_benefit_Info_Motto: Maximise NPS tax room before you lock the contribution.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_LearnText: If you pay rent but get no HRA, Section 80GG (old regime) allows a deduction equal to the least of ₹60,000/year, 25% of adjusted total income, or rent minus 10% of ATI.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_FormulaNote: Old regime only. Other eligibility (no house in the city, etc.) is simplified — confirm with your CA.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_AtiHint: Roughly total income after other Chapter VI-A deductions except 80GG.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_VerdictIneligib: 80GG needs no HRA and positive rent and ATI. Switch to HRA calculator if you receive HRA.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_ScenarioSubtitl: Lower rent vs base vs higher ATI
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Step_2: Confirm you do not receive HRA.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Purpose: Estimate Section 80GG deduction for tenants without HRA.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_WhyKnow: Self-employed and non-HRA employees often miss this old-regime benefit.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_RealLife: Filing ITR under old regime while paying rent without HRA.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Alt_Hra_Wh: You receive HRA from your employer.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Differenti: FinYatra shows all three 80GG limits and the binding one clearly.
Tool_80gg_rent_deduction_Info_Motto: No HRA? Check 80GG before you skip the rent claim.
Tool_in_hand_salary_LearnText: Enter CTC, basic %, regime, and city type. We estimate gross, employee PF, professional tax, and income tax to show monthly and annual in-hand — a simplified wrap of the full salary calculator.
Tool_in_hand_salary_FormulaNote: Illustrative. Real slips vary by state PT, PF wage ceiling, and exact tax computation.
Tool_in_hand_salary_BasicHint: Higher basic raises PF and can lower in-hand.
Tool_in_hand_salary_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower CTC vs base vs higher basic %
Tool_in_hand_salary_ExampleStep_2: Gross excludes employer PF and gratuity inside CTC.
Tool_in_hand_salary_ExampleStep_3: Deduct employee PF (~12% of basic) and PT.
Tool_in_hand_salary_ExampleStep_4: Estimate annual tax under new regime and divide by 12.
Tool_in_hand_salary_Info_Purpose: Convert CTC into an estimated monthly take-home salary.
Tool_in_hand_salary_Info_WhyKnow: Offers quote CTC; you need in-hand to budget.
Tool_in_hand_salary_Info_RealLife: Comparing job offers and basic-% trade-offs.
Tool_in_hand_salary_Info_Alt_Salary_When: Need HRA, rent, and detailed breakup.
Tool_in_hand_salary_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra gives a fast CTC→in-hand view with regime and basic % toggles.
Tool_debt_payoff_LearnText: Enter balance, rate, EMI, and optional extra payment. We simulate month-by-month payoff and show months saved and interest saved vs EMI alone. For one loan, snowball and avalanche are the same math.
Tool_debt_payoff_FormulaNote: If payment ≤ monthly interest, the loan never clears. Multi-debt ordering is educational for one loan.
Tool_debt_payoff_StrategyHint: With one loan both strategies produce the same schedule.
Tool_debt_payoff_VerdictNever: Your payment does not cover monthly interest — raise EMI or extra payment.
Tool_debt_payoff_ScenarioSubtitle: No extra vs your plan vs 2× extra
Tool_debt_payoff_ExampleStep_2: Without extra, simulate until balance clears.
Tool_debt_payoff_ExampleStep_4: Fewer months and lower total interest.
Tool_debt_payoff_Info_Purpose: See how extra payments shorten a loan and save interest.
Tool_debt_payoff_Info_WhyKnow: Small extras compound into large interest savings.
Tool_debt_payoff_Info_RealLife: Clearing a personal loan or credit-card balance faster.
Tool_debt_payoff_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows months and interest saved from extra payments on one loan.
Tool_debt_payoff_Info_Motto: Pay a little extra — finish a lot sooner.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Subtitle: SGB கூப்பன் vs ETF வரிக்குப் பின்
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_LearnText: Invest the same amount in SGB and Gold ETF. SGB earns 2.5% p.a. on issue price plus gold appreciation (maturity gain tax-free for individuals in this model). ETF tracks gold minus expense ratio and illustrative 12.5% LTCG on gains.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_FormulaNote: Educational. SGB early exit, spreads, and ETF tracking error are ignored. Confirm tax rules for your case.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_YearsHint: SGB tenure is typically 8 years to maturity.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_InterestNote: SGB interest over tenure ≈ {0}. Illustrative ETF LTCG ≈ {1}.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_ScenarioSubtitle: Flat gold vs your view vs +20% end price
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_ExampleStep_4: ETF grows with gold, minus ~0.5% expense/year, then 12.5% LTCG on gain.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_ExampleStep_5: Compare net SGB vs net ETF.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Step_4: Compute ETF net after expense and tax.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_Purpose: Compare post-cost returns of SGB and Gold ETF for the same outlay.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_WhyKnow: SGB’s 2.5% coupon and tax treatment often beat plain gold ETF over 8 years.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_RealLife: Choosing between RBI SGB issue and a gold ETF SIP/lump sum.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra pairs SGB’s 2.5% coupon with an ETF expense + LTCG net.
Tool_gold_etf_vs_sgb_Info_Motto: Same gold bet — different cash flows and tax.
Tool_fd_ladder_LearnText: Divide your amount equally across N FDs with tenures 1y, 2y, 3y… (or your shortest + step). See each rung’s maturity, total interest, and weighted-average maturity for liquidity planning.
Tool_fd_ladder_FormulaNote: Same rate on all rungs for simplicity. Real banks may offer different rates by tenure.
Tool_fd_ladder_ScenarioSubtitle: Fewer vs base vs more rungs
Tool_fd_ladder_ExampleStep_5: Sum maturities for total ladder value.
Tool_fd_ladder_Step_1: Enter total amount and rung count.
Tool_fd_ladder_Step_3: Split equally and compound each rung.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_Purpose: Build a ladder of FDs for rate capture and periodic liquidity.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_WhyKnow: A ladder avoids locking everything for one long tenure.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_RealLife: Parking emergency or goal money with yearly liquidity.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_Alt_Rd_When: Monthly deposits instead of lump sum.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_Alt_Budget_When: Planning what to ladder from surplus.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows each rung and the weighted-average maturity in one view.
Tool_fd_ladder_Info_Motto: Stagger maturities — keep liquidity without idle cash.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_LearnText: Enter annual CTC and remaining notice days. Buyout cost ≈ days × (CTC / 365). Use it to weigh joining a new role sooner versus serving notice.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_FormulaNote: Companies may use calendar days, working days, or gross monthly ÷ 30. This is an educational estimate — check your offer letter.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_DaysHint: Days left to serve or buy out.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_ScenarioSubtit: Half days vs base vs longer notice
Tool_notice_period_buyout_ExampleStep_5: Compare with half notice or full 90-day cases.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Abbreviat: Notice buyout — cost to leave early
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Purpose: Estimate the cash cost of buying out remaining notice.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_WhyKnow: Job switches often hinge on whether buyout is worth the earlier join date.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_RealLife: Negotiating early exit with current employer.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Alt_Hike_: Comparing offer hike vs buyout cost.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Alt_Budge: Can you afford the buyout from savings?
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Different: FinYatra turns remaining notice into a clear rupee buyout using daily CTC.
Tool_notice_period_buyout_Info_Motto: Price the early exit before you resign.
Tool_nri_fd_LearnText: Enter deposit, rate, tenure, and account type. NRE interest is treated as tax-free in India in this model; NRO interest has illustrative 30% TDS. Compare net maturity across account types.
Tool_nri_fd_FormulaNote: Educational. Actual NRE/NRO rates, DTAA relief, and TDS certificates vary by bank and residency — confirm with your bank/CA.
Tool_nri_fd_TaxNote_Nre: NRE FD interest is generally tax-free in India for NRIs (educational).
Tool_nri_fd_TaxNote_Nro: NRO interest is taxable; this model deducts illustrative TDS of {0}. Claim credit / DTAA as applicable.
Tool_nri_fd_ScenarioSubtitle: Other account type vs base vs longer tenure
Tool_nri_fd_ExampleStep_3: NRE: net = gross (no India tax in this model).
Tool_nri_fd_ExampleStep_4: NRO: TDS ≈ 30% of interest → lower net.
Tool_nri_fd_ExampleStep_5: Compare net maturity across account types.
Tool_nri_fd_Step_4: Apply NRO TDS note if needed.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_Purpose: Estimate NRE/NRO FD maturity and the NRO tax drag.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_WhyKnow: NRE vs NRO tax treatment can change net returns materially.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_RealLife: Parking repatriable or India-source funds as an NRI.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_Alt_Fd_When: Resident FD without NRI account types.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra contrasts NRE tax-free interest with NRO TDS on the same deposit.
Tool_nri_fd_Info_Motto: Same rate — different net after NRO tax.
Tool_dtaa_LearnText: Under DTAA / Section 90-style foreign tax credit, India usually allows credit up to the Indian tax on the same income. Excess foreign tax is typically not refundable here.
Tool_dtaa_FormulaNote: Educational only. Treaty rates, Form 67 timing, and TRC/10F rules vary by country — confirm with a CA.
Tool_dtaa_IndiaTaxHint: Illustrative Indian tax after treaty / slab on that income.
Tool_dtaa_ScenarioSubtitle: Lower vs base vs higher foreign tax paid
Tool_dtaa_ExampleStep_1: Foreign income ₹10,00,000; foreign tax ₹1,50,000; India tax ₹2,00,000.
Tool_dtaa_ExampleStep_5: If foreign were ₹2,50,000, FTC stays ₹2,00,000 and ₹50,000 stays uncredited.
Tool_dtaa_Step_1: Enter foreign income and tax paid abroad.
Tool_dtaa_Step_2: Enter illustrative India tax on the same income.
Tool_dtaa_Step_3: Credit = minimum of the two taxes.
Tool_dtaa_Step_4: See net India tax and any uncredited foreign tax.
Tool_dtaa_Info_Purpose: Estimate foreign tax credit against Indian tax on the same income.
Tool_dtaa_Info_WhyKnow: Double taxation relief is capped at Indian tax — excess foreign tax may not help.
Tool_dtaa_Info_RealLife: NRI salary, foreign dividends, Form 67 filing.
Tool_dtaa_Info_Goal_2: See net India tax after credit
Tool_dtaa_Info_Alt_Nri_When: For residential status and India-source income.
Tool_dtaa_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows FTC, net India tax, and uncredited foreign tax in one view.
Tool_dtaa_Info_Motto: Credit stops at Indian tax — plan treaties early.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_LearnText: Section 80DDB (old regime) allows a capped deduction for specified disease treatment. Limits are ₹40,000 generally and ₹1,00,000 for senior citizens.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_FormulaNote: Educational. Disease list is Rule 11DD; insurance reimbursements may reduce eligible expense.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Step_1: Enter medical expense for specified disease.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_Purpose: Estimate Section 80DDB deduction and tax saving.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_WhyKnow: Specified disease costs can reduce taxable income within fixed caps.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_RealLife: Cancer, neurological, chronic renal treatment bills.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_Differen: FinYatra isolates 80DDB limits and slab saving clearly.
Tool_80ddb_medical_expense_Info_Motto: Know the 80DDB cap before you claim.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_LearnText: Section 80U gives a fixed deduction to a resident individual with disability. Severe disability (≥80%) raises the deduction to ₹1,25,000 from ₹75,000.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_FormulaNot: Educational, old regime. Certificate under Rule 11D is required — amounts are fixed, not expense-based.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_Info_Purpo: Show fixed Section 80U deduction and tax saving.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_Info_WhyKn: 80U is a flat deduction — severity changes the amount.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_Info_RealL: ITR old regime with disability certificate.
Tool_80u_disability_deduction_Info_Diffe: FinYatra makes 80U severity bands and saving obvious.
Tool_ulip_surrender_LearnText: Surrendering a ULIP early can mean a gain or loss versus premiums paid. Tax treatment has changed over time — this tool flags when a review is often needed.
Tool_ulip_surrender_FormulaNote: Educational only. Actual ULIP tax depends on issue date, premium size, and current law — confirm with a CA.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Result_TaxableYes: May need tax review (short hold / high premium)
Tool_ulip_surrender_ExampleStep_5: Longer hold may change the flag — still confirm rules.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Step_2: Enter surrender value and years held.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Info_Purpose: Compare surrender value to premiums and note tax risk.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Info_WhyKnow: Early ULIP exits often surprise on charges and tax.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra links surrender gain with an educational tax flag.
Tool_ulip_surrender_Info_Motto: Check gain and tax before you surrender.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_LearnText: No-claim bonus reduces the own-damage premium when you have claim-free years. Third-party premium usually has no NCB.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_FormulaNote: Educational. Insurer NCB grids and OD rates vary — use your quote for exact numbers.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_Info_Purpose: Estimate NCB discount on motor OD premium.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_Info_WhyKnow: Claim-free years lower OD cost — TP stays separate.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_Info_Differenti: FinYatra isolates NCB math on OD plus TP.
Tool_motor_insurance_ncb_Info_Motto: Protect your NCB — it cuts OD cost.
Tool_under_construction_emi_LearnText: During construction you often pay simple interest on the amount disbursed. After possession, full EMI starts on the loan.
Tool_under_construction_emi_FormulaNote: Educational. Banks may use different disbursal schedules and capitalisation rules.
Tool_under_construction_emi_ExampleStep_: EMI calculated on full ₹50L for 240 months.
Tool_under_construction_emi_Step_1: Enter loan, rate, and pre-possession months.
Tool_under_construction_emi_Info_Purpose: Estimate pre-possession interest and post-possession EMI.
Tool_under_construction_emi_Info_WhyKnow: Construction loans cost interest before EMI starts.
Tool_under_construction_emi_Info_Differe: FinYatra splits pre-possession interest from post EMI.
Tool_under_construction_emi_Info_Motto: Delay in possession raises interest cost.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_LearnText: This simulator applies simple educational deltas for payment habit, utilisation, enquiries, and credit age. It is not CIBIL, Experian, or any bureau model.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_FormulaNote: For learning only. Real bureau models are proprietary and use full credit files.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_ScenarioSubt: Weak vs base vs strong habits
Tool_credit_score_simulator_ExampleSubti: 700 base · good pay · 35% util · 1 enquiry · 5 yrs
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_Purpose: Teach how habits can move an illustrative score.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_WhyKnow: Payment and utilisation dominate most score stories.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_RealLif: Before applying for a loan or card.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_Goal_3: Remember it is not a bureau score
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_Differe: FinYatra keeps the simulator clearly educational — not a bureau pull.
Tool_credit_score_simulator_Info_Motto: Pay on time and keep utilisation low.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_LearnTex: GST registration is generally required once aggregate turnover crosses the threshold for your category — often ₹20L (services), ₹40L (goods), or ₹10L (special category states).
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_FormulaN: Educational. Inter-state supply, e-commerce, and notified categories can require registration earlier.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_Scenario: Special vs your category vs goods
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_ExampleS: Same turnover as goods may still be below ₹40L.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_Info_Pur: Check turnover against common GST registration limits.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_Info_Why: Crossing the threshold usually means mandatory registration.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_Info_Dif: FinYatra compares 20L/40L/10L thresholds in one place.
Tool_gst_registration_threshold_Info_Mot: Know your GST threshold before you cross it.
Tool_international_equity_tax_LearnText: Many foreign shares and international FoFs are not taxed like listed Indian equity LTCG. This sketch applies your slab rate plus cess on the gain.
Tool_international_equity_tax_FormulaNot: Educational. Classification and rates change — confirm current treatment with a CA.
Tool_international_equity_tax_Info_Purpo: Estimate slab-style tax on foreign equity gains.
Tool_international_equity_tax_Info_WhyKn: Foreign equity often lacks listed-equity LTCG rates.
Tool_international_equity_tax_Info_Diffe: FinYatra highlights slab treatment for international equity gains.
Tool_international_equity_tax_Info_Motto: Foreign equity tax is often slab — plan for it.
Tool_reits_invits_tax_LearnText: REIT/InvIT payouts often mix interest, dividend, and other components with different tax notes. This sketch taxes components at your slab (with an optional dividend toggle).
Tool_reits_invits_tax_FormulaNote: Educational. Actual characterisation follows the distribution advice / tax statement.
Tool_reits_invits_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate tax on REIT/InvIT distribution components.
Tool_reits_invits_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Mixed components mean mixed tax outcomes.
Tool_reits_invits_tax_Info_Motto: Read the distribution split before you estimate tax.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_LearnText: Partnership firms are generally taxed at a flat 30% on taxable profit, with surcharge above ₹1 crore and health & education cess. Partner remuneration rules are simplified here.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_FormulaNote: Educational. Section 40(b) limits, AMT, and 194T TDS are not fully modelled.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_RemunerationHi: Shown for context; tax is computed on firm profit entered.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_Info_Purpose: Estimate flat firm-level tax with surcharge and cess.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Firms face flat 30% — surcharge kicks in above ₹1 Cr.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_Info_Different: FinYatra shows firm flat tax with surcharge band clearly.
Tool_partnership_firm_tax_Info_Motto: Firm tax is flat — watch the ₹1 Cr surcharge line.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_LearnText: PM Vishwakarma supports traditional artisans with a toolkit grant, training stipend, and concessional credit (illustrative max ₹3 lakh at ~5%). Enter optional loan details and training days to see cash benefits and EMI.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_FormulaNote: Scheme amounts and rates change by notification. Educational only — not a sanction letter.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_Purpose: Summarise grant, stipend, and optional toolkit credit EMI.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_WhyKnow: Know cash support and EMI before taking scheme credit.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_RealLife: Traditional trade artisans planning PM Vishwakarma benefits.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra pairs the ₹15K grant and stipend with optional EMI.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_Motto: See grant, stipend, and EMI in one place.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_ExampleStep_5: Compare grant-only and max ₹3L credit scenarios.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Step_1: Enter optional credit amount, rate, and months.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Step_3: Add toolkit grant + stipend as cash benefits.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Step_4: Cap credit at ₹3L and compute EMI.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_Alt_Emi_When: For any loan without scheme caps.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_Info_Alt_Budget_When: To fit EMI into cash flow.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_CappedNote: Requested {0} exceeds illustrative max {1} — EMI uses the capped amount.
Tool_pm_vishwakarma_ScenarioSubtitle: Grant only vs your credit vs max ₹3L
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_LearnText: Stand-Up India targets SC/ST and women entrepreneurs with bank loans typically between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore. Enter amount, rate, and tenure to see EMI on the amount clamped to that band.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_FormulaNote: Eligibility, margin money, and moratorium vary by bank. Educational only.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Info_Purpose: Estimate EMI within the ₹10L–₹1Cr scheme band.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Info_WhyKnow: Band and EMI decide whether the project cash flow works.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Info_RealLife: SC/ST or women-led greenfield enterprise loans.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Info_Differenti: FinYatra clamps to the Stand-Up India band before EMI.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Info_Motto: Plan EMI inside the scheme band.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_ExampleStep_5: Compare min and max band EMIs in scenarios.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_Step_1: Enter loan amount, rate, and months.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_SchemeNote: Stand-Up India is aimed at SC/ST and women entrepreneurs — confirm bank eligibility and documents.
Tool_stand_up_india_loan_ScenarioSubtitl: Min ₹10L vs your amount vs max ₹1Cr
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Subtitle: அதே ₹ → வரிக்குப் பின் டெட் MF vs FD
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_LearnText: Both debt mutual funds and FDs are often taxed at your slab. Enter the same principal, years, and expected rates to compare post-tax maturity values.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_FormulaNote: Debt MF taxation rules and FD TDS vary. Educational comparison only.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Info_Purpose: Compare same-rupee debt MF and FD after tax.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Info_WhyKnow: Pre-tax rates can mislead when both are slab-taxed.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Info_RealLife: Parking surplus in debt funds vs bank FD.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows post-tax nets side by side.
Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Info_Motto: Compare after tax, not just the headline rate.

Tool_debt_mf_vs_fd_Step_3: Apply slab tax on gains / interest.

Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_LearnTe: Section 80E allows deduction of the entire education-loan interest (no amount cap) for up to 8 years from the year repayment starts — typically old regime. Enter interest paid and slab to see tax saving.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Formula: Only interest qualifies (not principal). Confirm regime and lender eligibility.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Info_Pu: Estimate full interest deduction and tax saving.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Info_Wh: 80E has no rupee cap but a time window.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Info_Di: FinYatra applies the 8-year window before saving.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Info_Mo: Full interest deduction — check the window.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Example: Compare lower slab and outside-window cases.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Step_1: Enter interest paid in the FY.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Step_2: Enter tax slab and years since repayment start.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Step_3: If within 8 years, deduction = full interest.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_YearsHi: 80E window is typically 8 years from repayment start.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Outside: Deduction is shown as zero after the illustrative 8-year window.
Tool_80e_education_loan_interest_Scenari: Lower slab vs base vs outside window
Tool_section_10_exemptions_LearnText: Enter common Section 10 exemption amounts (HRA, LTA, leave encashment, gratuity, other). The tool sums them and applies an educational soft cap — not a full exemption matrix.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_FormulaNote: Actual limits differ by section and regime. Educational sum only.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Info_Purpose: Sum common Sec 10 exemption amounts educationally.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Info_WhyKnow: Scattered exemptions are easy to under-count.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Info_RealLife: Salaried employees under old regime planning exemptions.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Info_Differen: FinYatra totals common Sec 10 inputs with a soft cap.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Info_Motto: Add up exemptions before you file.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Step_1: Enter HRA, LTA, leave, gratuity, other.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_Step_4: Review total exempt vs raw sum.
Tool_section_10_exemptions_ScenarioSubti: HRA only vs your mix vs +gratuity
Tool_gst_hsn_code_LearnText: This is not a full HSN database. Choose an illustrative GST rate category (0/5/12/18/28%), enter taxable value, and see GST with intra-state or inter-state split.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_FormulaNote: Always verify the official HSN/SAC rate for your goods or services.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_Purpose: Estimate GST from a category rate, not a full HSN lookup.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_WhyKnow: Wrong rate on invoices creates compliance risk.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_RealLife: Quick invoice GST check by rate slab.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra uses category presets with CGST/SGST/IGST split.
Tool_gst_hsn_code_Info_Motto: Pick the rate band, then verify the official HSN.
Tool_rent_increase_LearnText: Enter current monthly rent, expected annual increase %, and years to see final monthly rent and total rent paid over the period.
Tool_rent_increase_FormulaNote: Actual increases depend on agreement and local rules. Educational projection only.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Purpose: Project escalating rent and total outflow.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_WhyKnow: Small % increases compound into large totals.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_RealLife: Lease renewals and long-stay rent planning.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows final rent and cumulative paid together.
Tool_rent_increase_Info_Motto: See where rent goes before you renew.
Tool_rent_increase_ExampleStep_4: Sum annual rents for total paid.
Tool_rent_increase_ExampleStep_5: Compare lower and higher increase scenarios.
Tool_rent_increase_Step_4: Sum total rent paid over the period.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_LearnText: Enter Equity (E), Corporate (C), and Government (G) percentages plus expected returns. The tool checks that weights sum to ~100% and computes a weighted expected return.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_FormulaNote: Age-linked equity caps and Auto choice rules are not fully modelled. Educational only.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Purpose: Validate weights and blend expected return.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Different: FinYatra validates 100% and shows blend instantly.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_Info_Motto: Weights that add to 100 — then see the blend.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_InvalidNote: Current sum is {0}. Adjust E/C/G so they add to 100%.
Tool_nps_asset_allocation_ScenarioSubtit: Conservative vs your mix vs aggressive
Tool_health_insurance_port_LearnText: Portability can carry forward waiting-period credit already served. Enter original waiting months, months completed, and current vs new sum insured to see waiting left and any cover increase (gap) note.
Tool_health_insurance_port_FormulaNote: IRDAI rules and insurer practices vary. Educational only — not a porting application.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Purpose: Estimate waiting left and cover-gap note on port.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_WhyKnow: Porting without checking waiting credit can surprise claims.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_RealLife: Switching insurers while keeping PED waiting credit.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Differen: FinYatra shows leftover waiting and SI gap together.
Tool_health_insurance_port_Info_Motto: Know waiting left before you port.
Tool_health_insurance_port_ExampleStep_5: Same-SI port keeps waiting credit without gap.

Tool_health_insurance_port_Step_3: Enter current and new sum insured.

Tool_health_insurance_port_ScenarioSubti: Earlier port vs base vs same SI
Tool_variable_pay_tax_LearnText: Enter fixed pay, variable/bonus, and marginal slab. See tax with and without variable (plus cess) and the extra tax attributable to variable pay.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_FormulaNote: Simplified flat slab on amounts — not a full progressive computation.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Purpose: Compare tax with and without variable at slab.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Bonus months can spike TDS unexpectedly.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_RealLife: CTC with performance bonus / variable pay.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra isolates the extra tax on variable.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_Info_Motto: See what variable really costs after tax.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_ExampleStep_3: Tax with variable on ₹15L at same rate.
Tool_variable_pay_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: No variable vs base vs +50% variable
Tool_swp_tax_LearnText: Enter SWP withdrawal, average cost per unit, current NAV, and holding months. Units redeemed = withdrawal / NAV; cost basis = units × cost; capital gains tax is estimated on the gain.
Tool_swp_tax_FormulaNote: FIFO lots and ₹1.25L LTCG exemption across funds are simplified. Educational only.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Purpose: Rough CG tax on each SWP redemption.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_WhyKnow: SWP is not tax-free — only the gain portion is taxed.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_RealLife: Monthly SWP from equity mutual funds in retirement.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra converts withdrawal to units and estimates CG tax.
Tool_swp_tax_Info_Motto: Tax the gain, not the whole withdrawal.
Tool_swp_tax_ExampleStep_5: Compare STCG vs longer LTCG scenarios.
Tool_swp_tax_SlabHint: Used mainly for STCG context; equity LTCG uses illustrative CG rates.
